title: Computer malware
description: Check your understanding of computer malware, computer viruses, and keylogging in this set of free practice questions designed for AP Computer Science Principles students.
topic_path: Going deeper with recognizing and avoiding online scams

assessment_items.question_content:
An IT manager for a company wants to make sure that the corporate computers are secure, so they install antivirus software on every machine.

**In what way does the antivirus software protect the computers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The antivirus software removes all viruses from files. It will not protect against Trojan horses or worms, however.
The antivirus software detects and deflects DDoS attacks.
The antivirus software checks for known malware and takes action to remove or repair affected files.
The antivirus software make sure that users only visit websites that are secured over HTTPS.

assessment_items.question_content:
The administration of a high school decides to install antivirus software on all of the school computers.

**What can the antivirus software protect the computers from?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The antivirus software can protect the computers from any malware that it is able to successfully detect.
The antivirus software can protect the computers entirely from all computer viruses.
The antivirus software can protect the computers entirely from all forms of malware, like Trojan horses, worms, and viruses.
The antivirus software can protect the computer from a DDoS attack.

assessment_items.question_content:
ZeuS is malware that is typically used to steal banking data from a computer's users by installing a key logger and sending the logged data to the attackers.

**What best describes how antivirus software can protect against ZeuS?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

*Source: [Wikipedia: Zeus (malware)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zeus_\(malware\))*

choices.content: Antivirus software can warn the user not to use banking websites.
Antivirus software can scan the files on the drive and notify users of files that look like the ZeuS malware.
Antivirus software can prevent the user from ever downloading any malware.
Antivirus software can block network requests that are coming from a known ZeuS botnet.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following algorithms is most likely to be found in a computer virus?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An algorithm that copies the virus program into a different file
An algorithm that records all of the keys typed by a user
An algorithm that monitors the data sent over the Internet from the user
An algorithm that sends emails to all of the user's contacts

assessment_items.question_content:
A user complains that their anti-virus software claimed to have eliminated a virus, but it popped up again the next day.

**What's the most likely explanation for why a computer virus may be hard to eliminate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A virus can copy itself into multiple files on the operating system.
A virus encrypts the file that it's hiding in, making it harder to find.
A virus can only be discovered if the anti-virus software has the correct password to open its file.
A virus can change the permissions on its executable program file so that the anti-virus program can't fully delete it.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is a computer virus more dangerous than other types of malware?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A virus can decrypt any data that has been encrypted on the user's hard drive.
A virus can make copies of itself, including copying itself into an existing file.
A virus has access to the user's input devices, so it can see what they type and how they move their cursor.
A virus can access the internal system processes of a computer so that it can change how the operating system works.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following snippet is from a 2020 news article:

> "On March 18, we uncovered an email campaign that pushed victims into unwittingly downloading an invasive keylogger called Agent Tesla."

**What is the primary danger of accidentally downloading a keylogger?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A keylogger will replicate itself into other files on the computer.
A keylogger will prevent the user from typing anything on their computer.
A keylogger will share the secret keys of the user with other users on the network.
A keylogger will record every key stroke and upload the data to an attacker.

assessment_items.question_content:
BBC News wrote an article with this headline:

"HP laptops found to have hidden keylogger"

**After reading that headline, what should HP laptop owners be most concerned about?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The keylogger could be using up their computing power to mine for cryptocurrency.
The keylogger could be overwriting their files with random generated text.
The keylogger could be sending emails to all of their contacts with malware attached.
The keylogger could be recording what they type and sending the logs to a server.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following snippet is from a 2017 article on an information security news site.

> "A Virginia man pleaded guilty on Friday to charges of aiding and abetting computer intrusion, accused of writing a keylogger that sold to over 3,000 people and infected 16,000 victims."

**In what way does a keylogger aid computer intrusion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A keylogger decrypts all the data that a user sends over the Internet, even if they are using a secured connection.
A keylogger finds all of the text documents on a hard drive and sends them to the attacker.
A keylogger accesses a user's password manager, harvests all the passwords, and sends them in plain text over the Internet.
A keylogger records everything a user types and uploads the data to a server.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the difference between malware and a software bug?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Malware is designed to intentionally inflict damage on a computer; a software bug is an accident.
A piece of malware has a huge impact; a software bug only affects a few users.
Malware is a part of computer hardware, not a part of computer software.
Malware is only in code that is sent over the Internet, while bugs can happen in any software.

assessment_items.question_content:
A teacher in a computer science classroom is writing guidelines for her classes. She would like to explicitly forbid the creation of malware.

**Which of these rules bans the creation of malware?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: "Don't create software that (unknowingly or not) damages the user's data or computer in some way."
"Don't create a program that intentionally damages a computing system or takes unauthorized control of it."
"Don't write code that knows how to make copies of itself."
"Don't develop an application that asks a user for sensitive information such as their password or credit card number."

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these statements about malware are true?**

👁️**Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Malware can affect desktops, laptops, phones, and servers.
A virus is a type of computer malware that only affects web servers.
Malware is a term that means the same thing as computer virus.
A virus is a type of computer malware, but there are other types of malware.
Malware is a type of computer virus, but there are other types of viruses.
